title: Create baseline test script
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

I created a script to be used for baseline testing against new Buildah kits.  This is based on the tutorial created by @ipbabble for the Buildah project, although I replaced his httpd container with a whalesays.

comments.body: LGTM, should we plug this in so that it runs in papr? Travis?

Heck yeah!

Ahem ... I mean, what a swell idea.

On Mon, Nov 20, 2017 at 7:15 AM Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> LGTM, should we plug this in so that it runs in papr? Travis?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/328#issuecomment-345707413>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ABPpgcX1y2gLXdocWwZQma8JBFLDRaIJks5s4YlvgaJpZM4QjgO0>
> .
>
-- 
Sent from Gmail Mobile on iPhone

I didn't add this to papr/travis as I was concerned about the docker install/deinstall. Also I think much of the functions that are tested here are in the *.bats tests that get run in papr/travis.  The main purpose of this script was just to have something handy that I could slap on a new VM with a new rev of Buildah on it and run it quickly.  I could instead just use the current *.bats tests, it just takes longer to clone and setup those.

Ah, makes sense. BTW I am going to do some more work on this area. So I
hopefully will have some more tests as well as tutorials and also maybe
some man page updates.

William

William Henry
Senior Consulting Software Engineer
Red Hat
Colorado, USA.

On Mon, Nov 20, 2017 at 4:26 PM, Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

What I actually think we need is a mechanism to build an rpm, and then run this script after installing the RPM.  Perhaps on a clean VM.  That would get us to the point that we verify that we have no known regression in the thing we ship to users.
@rhatdan I think your rpm script sounds great, but I think it should be a separate script that calls this one.  Can we merge this one in for now and I'll work on the rpm script too?
